steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Change the sign of every term in the second polynomial. The negative sign applies to $9x^3$, $3x^2$, and $-2x$:
$$(7x^3-4x^2+5)-(9x^3+3x^2-2x)$$
$$=7x^3-4x^2+5-9x^3-3x^2+2x$$

steps.title: Group like terms
steps.content: Now combine terms with the same power of $x$. The cubic terms are $7x^3$ and $-9x^3$, the quadratic terms are $-4x^2$ and $-3x^2$, the linear term is $2x$, and the constant is $5$.

steps.title: Combine by degree
steps.content: Evaluate each group: $$(7x^3-9x^3)+(-4x^2-3x^2)+2x+5$$ $$=-2x^3-7x^2+2x+5$$ The answer is B.
